goal: Design-time security audit of invariants-audit feature
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Skill file exists at skills/invariants-audit/SKILL.md following size budget (<=120 prose lines, sibling refs via [See reference:] convention, stop conditions footer required)
AC1a: init.sh and upgrade.sh install to .claude/skills/invariants-audit/ with byte-identity sync tests verifying source/installed parity for SKILL.md and review-categories.md

AC2: Skill teaches 5 review categories — state-machine and transition bugs, blocked/rejected/retry/stale-state paths, spec vs implementation vs tests vs hooks contradictions, fixture-template-validator mismatches, tests that prove syntax/structure but not behavior
AC3: Skill guides 5-step invariant analysis — (1) identify invariant, (2) where encoded, (3) where enforced, (4) where tested, (5) failure paths

AC4: Claude commands reference the skill — review.md, architect.md, security-gate.md, test-design.md each have Skill References table row for invariants-audit pointing to skills/invariants-audit/SKILL.md

AC5: Codex reviewer prompts contain ## Invariant Checks section with **Apply when:** trigger and at least one checklist item — review-code.md, review-prd.md, review-architecture.md, review-test-design.md

AC6: Wiring contract tests verify: Claude command Skill References rows for invariants-audit; Codex reviewer ## Invariant Checks sections; Apply when: triggers; ### Invariant Analysis marker inside each Invariant Checks section; SKILL.md structural anchors (## Review Categories, ## Invariant Review Method, ## When to Use); stop conditions footer; sibling reference resolution
AC7: Skill documents when to use invariants-audit vs normal review with clear trigger conditions (workflow logic, state transitions, safety checks, test architecture changes)
known_risks: M2 category-2 heading regex uses a complex co-occurrence pattern; if the implementer writes a heading with only one of the four category-2 terms the test will still fail correctly, but the regex is harder to read. Review if it causes confusion during GREEN phase.

L2 checklist tests fail at the section-not-found guard (correct RED), but will need the section to also contain a '- ' list item after the Apply when: trigger — implementer must add at least one list item, not just the trigger line.
